title: UK and 9 European allies sign JEF naval force statement of intent
summary: The United Kingdom, Netherlands, the five Nordic states and the three Baltic states signed a statement of intent to form a multinational maritime force under the British-led Joint Expeditionary Force complementing NATO and aimed at countering Russian threats in the High North, North Atlantic and Baltic regions.
contents:
# UK and 9 European allies sign JEF naval force statement of intent

## Coalition Composition
On April 30, the United Kingdom together with the Netherlands, five Nordic countries (Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden) and three Baltic states (Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania) signed a joint statement of intent under the British-led Joint Expeditionary Force (JEF) to stand up a multinational maritime force complementing NATO Maritime Command activity. The agreement signals a deepening of regional naval cooperation outside the formal NATO command structure.

## Operational Concept
The new force will train and operate together across the High North, North Atlantic and Baltic regions, with command potentially exercised from the United Kingdom's maritime headquarters at Northwood. JEF participants intend the formation to provide rapid maritime response options against Russian sub-surface, surface and grey-zone threats including undersea cable interference, suspicious shadow-fleet movements and incursions near critical national infrastructure.

## Strategic Context
The announcement coincides with continued Russian Black Sea Fleet attrition, increased Northern Fleet activity in the GIUK gap, and Russian probing operations near Baltic and Nordic infrastructure. NATO Maritime Command framed the JEF maritime force as additive to Standing NATO Maritime Groups rather than competitive, reinforcing collective deterrence at the Alliance's northern and eastern flanks.

## Tags
